mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in payroll employment signals expanding household income and lower recession risk, causing investors to raise expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS 3-month changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of triggered observations over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above its 6-month average by at least 0.25pp, tighter policy raises discount rates faster than near-term earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 6-month moving average by >= 0.25pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS tightening episodes have mean 60-day S&P 500 forward returns greater than or equal to unconditional mean returns in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and payrolls signals that consumer demand is expanding with income support, causing upward revisions to earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds its prior 6-month growth and PAYEMS 3-month growth exceeds its prior 6-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if the joint acceleration signal does not outperform either single-series acceleration signal by at least 10 bps average 20-day return over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in industrial production signals weakening factory output and operating leverage pressure, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside breaks are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations across a 5-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices while the policy rate remains pinned near zero signals a future tightening risk and margin pressure, causing equity risk premia to rise and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.50%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL acceleration and low FEDFUNDS has nonnegative estimated effect on 60-day returns in three consecutive annual walk-forward refits.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in housing starts signals stronger construction activity, durable goods demand, and credit-sensitive confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside breaks have a 60-day forward return hit rate below 50% or a mean return below the unconditional mean over any 4-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment-rate increase after a high starting level signals rising recession probability and weaker household income, causing equity investors to de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 3-month average by >= 0.2pp while UNRATE is above 6%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE deterioration episodes have positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When real output growth is weak and retail sales contract, the economy lacks both aggregate and household demand momentum, causing downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth is below 1% annualized and RSAFS 3-month percent change crosses below 0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-GDPC1 and falling-RSAFS signal has average 20-day returns greater than or equal to zero in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.
